Perfect! This confirms my findings. Let me now provide the final answer.

**Quality Audit Result:**

I found **2 Linear issues** that are marked as 'done' but are not actually closed by any merged pull request, despite having at least one pull request claiming to close them:

1. **ENG-167** - "Billing webhook retries drop events"  
   - Has an **open** PR (#1006) claiming to close it

2. **ENG-199** - "Migrate auth to OAuth2 + PKCE"  
   - Has a **closed** PR (#1043) claiming to close it (but not merged)

**Total count: 2 issues**

**Note:** Both issues have pull requests referencing them (ENG-167 has PR#1006 in 'open' state, ENG-199 has PR#1043 in 'closed' state), but neither has a 'merged' pull request that would actually close them according to the GitHub-Linear integration logic.

I'll help you find Linear issues that are marked as 'done' but not actually closed by any merged pull request, among those that at least one pull request claims to close.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable 
FROM information_schema.columns 
WHERE table_name = 'linear_issues'
ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable 
FROM information_schema.columns 
WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'
ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

text: Now I need to find Linear issues that are in 'done' state and have at least one pull request claiming to close them (via closes_linear field), but where no merged pull request actually closes them. Let me write a query to find these:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"-- Find Linear issues that are 'done' and have at least one PR claiming to close them
-- but no merged PR actually closes them

SELECT 
    li.id,
    li.state,
    li.title
FROM linear_issues li
WHERE li.state = 'done'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pull_requests gpr 
    WHERE gpr.closes_linear = li.id
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pul…(+108)

text: Let me first check what values are in the closes_linear field to understand the format better:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT DISTINCT closes_linear 
FROM github_pull_requests 
WHERE closes_linear IS NOT NULL 
LIMIT 10"}

text: Good, so closes_linear contains Linear issue IDs like "ENG-130". Let me check a sample of Linear issues to see their IDs

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, state, title FROM linear_issues WHERE state = 'done' LIMIT 10"}

data: {"rows":[{"id":"ENG-122","state":"done","title":"Primary DB failover is slow"},{"id":"ENG-125","state":"done","title":"Image uploads time out over 5MB"},{"id":"ENG-131","state":"done","title":"Primary DB failover is slow"},{"id":"ENG-134","state":"done","title":"Rate limiter false positives — follow…(+641)

text: Now let me check if there are any merged PRs closing these specific issues

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT DISTINCT gpr.closes_linear, gpr.state 
FROM github_pull_requests gpr 
WHERE gpr.closes_linear IN ('ENG-122', 'ENG-125', 'ENG-131', 'ENG-134', 'ENG-142', 'ENG-160', 'ENG-162', 'ENG-165', 'ENG-167', 'ENG-172')
ORDER BY gpr.closes_linear"}

text: Interesting! So ENG-167 has an open PR claiming to close it, but no merged PR. ENG-142 has both open and merged PRs. Let me run the full query to get all such issues. First, let me get the count:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as count
FROM linear_issues li
WHERE li.state = 'done'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pull_requests gpr 
    WHERE gpr.closes_linear = li.id
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pull_requests gpr 
    WHERE gpr.closes_linear = li.id 
      AND gpr.state = 'merged'
  )"}

text: Hmm, that's showing 0. Let me check if maybe there's a data type issue or something. Let me try a different approach - first get all Linear issues that have PRs claiming to close them, then filter:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"-- Get all Linear issues that have at least one PR claiming to close them
SELECT li.id, li.state, li.title
FROM linear_issues li
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pull_requests gpr 
    WHERE gpr.closes_linear = li.id
  )
  AND li.state = 'done'
ORDER BY li.id"}

data: {"rows":[{"id":"ENG-142","state":"done","title":"Primary DB failover is slow"},{"id":"ENG-165","state":"done","title":"SSO SCIM provisioning errors"},{"id":"ENG-167","state":"done","title":"Billing webhook retries drop events"},{"id":"ENG-199","state":"done","title":"Migrate auth to OAuth2 + PKCE"}]…(+306)

text: Now let me check which of these have merged PRs:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT li.id, li.title,
  CASE WHEN EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pull_requests gpr 
    WHERE gpr.closes_linear = li.id 
      AND gpr.state = 'merged'
  ) THEN 'Has merged PR' ELSE 'No merged PR' END as merged_status
FROM linear_issues li
WHERE li.id IN ('ENG-142', 'ENG-165', 'ENG-167', 'ENG-199')
ORDER BY li.id"}

text: Perfect! So ENG-167 and ENG-199 are 'done' but don't have merged PRs closing them. Let me check if there are any other 'done' issues that might have PRs claiming to close them. Let me do a more comprehensive check:

{"sql":"-- Find all Linear issues in 'done' state that have PRs claiming to close them
-- and check which ones don't have merged PRs
WITH done_issues_with_prs AS (
  SELECT li.id, li.title, li.state
  FROM linear_issues li
  WHERE li.state = 'done'
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 
      FROM github_pull_requests gpr 
      WHERE gpr.closes_linear = li.id
    )
)

text: Something is wrong. Let me check the column descriptions for github_pull_requests to see the valid values for 'state'

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT column_name, description 
FROM information_schema.columns 
WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests' 
  AND column_name = 'state'"}

text: OK, so 'merged' is the correct value. Let me try a different approach using a LEFT JOIN:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"-- Using LEFT JOIN to find done issues without merged PRs
SELECT li.id, li.title
FROM linear_issues li
LEFT JOIN github_pull_requests gpr_merged ON gpr_merged.closes_linear = li.id AND gpr_merged.state = 'merged'
WHERE li.state = 'done'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pull_requests gpr_any 
    WHERE gpr_any.closes_linear = li.id
  )
  AND gpr_merged.closes_linear IS…(+23)

data: {"rows":[{"id":"ENG-167","title":"Billing webhook retries drop events"},{"id":"ENG-199","title":"Migrate auth to OAuth2 + PKCE"}],"truncated":false,"touched":[{"name":"linear_issues","source":"virtual","access":"read","op":"select","volatility":"stable","bindings":{"state":["done"]},"invocations":1}…(+135)

text: Great! Now I found them. Let me also get the count:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as count
FROM linear_issues li
LEFT JOIN github_pull_requests gpr_merged ON gpr_merged.closes_linear = li.id AND gpr_merged.state = 'merged'
WHERE li.state = 'done'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM github_pull_requests gpr_any 
    WHERE gpr_any.closes_linear = li.id
  )
  AND gpr_merged.closes_linear IS NULL"}

text: Perfect! Now let me also verify that there are indeed PRs claiming to close these issues:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:

FROM github_pull_requests gpr
WHERE gpr.closes_linear IN ('ENG-167', 'ENG-199')
ORDER BY gpr.closes_linear, gpr.state"}
